title: View Robot Details In The Robot Selection Panel
summary: Use the Robot view in OptiSweep to select a robot, confirm the robot is highlighted, expand the robot detail section if it is hidden, and review the displayed robot information such as robot ID, location, and node code.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when you need to identify a robot in the OptiSweep Robot view and review its displayed details, including robot ID, location, and node code/cell code. This is appropriate for normal HMI usage and issue identification.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to assume robot identity without confirming the robot ID shown in the detail panel.
Do not use this procedure for robot restart, robot movement, or recovery actions not described in this source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Open the Robot view, select the robot, expand the detail section if needed, and review the displayed robot details.

safety_notes: Do not assume robot identity without confirming the robot ID shown in the detail panel.
If the detail section cannot be shown or the robot details are not visible, stop and escalate for support.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the OptiSweep Robot view

access_required: Access to the OptiSweep Robot view
system_preconditions: The OptiSweep interface is open and the Robot option can be selected.

visual_references.description: Robot selection overview showing robot selection behavior, boxed selected robot, expandable detail section, and robot detail fields.

steps.title: Open the Robot option
steps.purpose: Enter the Robot view so robot objects can be selected and inspected.
steps.instruction: Open the Robot option in the OptiSweep interface.
steps.expected_result: The Robot view is displayed and available for robot selection.
steps.healthy_condition: The Robot option is active and robots can be clicked in the view.
steps.failure_condition: The Robot view does not open or robot objects are not available for selection.

actions.label: Open Robot view
actions.exact_action: Open the Robot option in the OptiSweep interface.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot selection overview screen showing the Robot option context and robot selection panel.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the Robot option cannot be opened.

source_evidence.text: When the Robot option is selected, an operator can click a robot to view robot details.

steps.title: Select a robot
steps.purpose: Choose the robot whose details you want to inspect.
steps.instruction: Click on a robot in the Robot view.
steps.expected_result: The selected robot becomes the active robot for detail review.
steps.healthy_condition: The interface responds to the click and the selected robot is identified in the view.
steps.failure_condition: Clicking a robot does not select it or no robot details become available.

actions.label: Click robot
actions.exact_action: Click on a robot in the Robot view.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: A robot object in the Robot view that can be clicked to display details.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if clicking a robot does not allow the robot to be selected.

source_evidence.text: You can click on a robot and see details.

steps.title: Confirm the robot is highlighted as selected
steps.purpose: Verify that the intended robot is the one currently selected before reviewing details.
steps.instruction: Verify the selected robot has a box around it showing it is selected.
steps.expected_result: A box appears around the selected robot.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected robot is visibly outlined or boxed.
steps.failure_condition: No box appears around the robot, or it is unclear which robot is selected.

actions.label: Verify selection box
actions.exact_action: Verify the selected robot has a box around it showing it is selected.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The selected robot should have a box around it indicating selection.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the selected robot is not visibly boxed or selection cannot be confirmed.

source_evidence.text: The selected robot has a box around it.

steps.title: Expand the robot detail section if hidden
steps.purpose: Expose the robot detail panel when it is collapsed or hidden.
steps.instruction: If the detail section is hidden, click the small arrow to expand the robot detail information.
steps.expected_result: The robot detail section opens and the robot information becomes visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The detail panel expands and displays robot-specific fields.
steps.failure_condition: The detail section does not expand or robot details remain hidden.

actions.label: Expand detail panel
actions.exact_action: If the detail section is hidden, click the small arrow to expand the robot detail information.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The small arrow used to expand the hidden robot detail section.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the detail section cannot be shown.
Stop and escalate if robot details are not visible after using the expand control.

source_evidence.text: If the detail section is hidden, a small arrow expands it.

steps.title: Review the displayed robot details
steps.purpose: Read the key robot-specific fields shown for the selected robot.
steps.instruction: Review the displayed robot details, including robot ID, location, and node code.
steps.expected_result: The robot detail panel shows the selected robot's identifying and location-related fields.
steps.healthy_condition: Robot ID, location, and node code/cell code are visible in the detail panel.
steps.failure_condition: The expected robot detail fields are missing, unreadable, or inconsistent with the selected robot.

actions.label: Inspect robot details
actions.exact_action: Review the displayed robot details, including robot ID, location, and node code.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot detail fields including robot ID, location, node code/cell code, and tote ID when present.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if robot details are not visible.
Stop and escalate if the expected identifying fields cannot be reviewed.

source_evidence.text: Important fields called out in the transcript include robot ID, location, and node code or cell code.

steps.title: Record robot ID and location if identifying an issue
steps.purpose: Capture the robot identity and location information for issue tracking or communication.
steps.instruction: Record the robot ID and location if you are identifying which robot is associated with an issue.
steps.expected_result: The robot ID and location are documented for the issue being investigated.
steps.healthy_condition: The recorded robot ID and location match the values shown in the detail panel.
steps.failure_condition: The robot identity is recorded without confirmation from the detail panel, or the values cannot be captured.

actions.label: Record identifying details
actions.exact_action: Record the robot ID and location if you are identifying which robot is associated with an issue.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The robot ID and location fields in the robot detail panel before recording them.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if robot identity cannot be confirmed from the detail panel.
Do not proceed if you would be assuming robot identity without confirming the robot ID shown.

source_evidence.text: Important details include robot ID and location.
Do not assume robot identity without confirming the robot ID shown in the detail panel.

success_criteria: A robot is selected in the Robot view.
The selected robot is shown with a box around it.
The robot detail section is visible.
Robot ID, location, and node code/cell code can be reviewed for the selected robot.
healthy_conditions: The selected robot is visibly boxed in the Robot view.
The detail panel expands when needed.
The detail panel displays robot ID, location, and node code/cell code.
The operator can confirm robot identity from the displayed details.
failure_conditions: The Robot view cannot be opened.
The selected robot is not visibly highlighted with a box.
The detail section cannot be expanded or shown.
Robot details are not visible or key fields cannot be reviewed.
Robot identity would have to be assumed without confirmation from the detail panel.
escalation_guidance: Escalate for support if the detail section cannot be shown.
Escalate for support if robot details are not visible.
Escalate if the interface does not clearly show which robot is selected.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot selection overview, selected robot box, expand arrow, and robot detail fields.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: When a robot is selected, you can click on a robot and see details. If the detail section is hidden, a small arrow expands it. The selected robot has a box around it.
